reasoning: I am voting for Shantanu again. What choice do I have? The papers have not come, my grandson's belly is still not full, and my knees still pain me. But Shantanu is Matua—he is one of us—and Harichand Thakur's people do not have many friends in Delhi. The TMC man, he has switched sides like a leaf in the wind; the Left, they do not know our faces. Shantanu has not kept his promise, this is true. But perhaps if he wins again, if he is given another chance... I cannot sit at home and do nothing. I will vote for him, though I no longer believe in miracles.
primary_drivers: Unfulfilled CAA/citizenship promise but no credible alternative
Resignation to limited political options for displaced minorities
